Bank Statements

Bank Statements (/bank-statements) allows PDF bank and credit card statements to be uploaded and processed by the LLM. Each transaction in the statement becomes a DocumentLine with a suggested GL account code and, where possible, a link to an outstanding sales invoice. The page is fully custom (not HasCrudForm).

Document types

Two document types are registered in config/documents.php:

Type keyLabelPrefixAuto-number
bank_statementBank StatementBSTMTNo — taken from the statement itself
credit_card_statementCredit Card StatementCCSTMTNo — taken from the statement itself

Both default to status received. The document_number column is nullable; if the LLM cannot extract a statement number the field is left blank.

Upload flow

  1. User clicks Upload Statement, selects one or more PDF files.
  2. Selects the contra account — the GL bank or credit card account this statement belongs to (stored as contra_account_id on Document).
  3. Optionally selects a bank template to pass layout hints to the LLM.
  4. Each file is stored via Spatie MediaLibrary on the source_document collection, a Document record is created with type bank_statement or credit_card_statement, and ProcessBankStatementDocument is dispatched.

Processing pipeline

BankStatementProcessingService::process(Document $document, ?string $userHint = null) (in app/Modules/Core/Services/) orchestrates:

  1. DocumentTextExtractor — extracts text from the PDF.
  2. LlmService::extractBankStatement() — calls the Claude API with resources/views/prompts/bank-statement-extraction.blade.php. Layout hints from the BankTemplate and any user hint are included. Returns an ExtractedBankStatement DTO containing header metadata and an array of ExtractedBankTransaction objects.
  3. The Document record is updated with extracted header fields (bank name, account name, statement number, period, opening/closing balances, currency). Balance reconciliation is checked and recorded in metadata.balance_reconciled.
  4. Outstanding sales invoices are pre-indexed by document_number (status sent or partially_paid).
  5. One DocumentLine is created per transaction. Amounts are stored as signed values (negative = debit/expense). If the LLM suggests an account code, it is resolved to an account_id. If the LLM suggests a matching invoice number, a linked_document_id is set.
  6. Document totals are recalculated and an llm_extracted activity is logged.

Like InvoiceProcessingService, this service only appends lines. Any reprocess must delete existing lines first — DocumentService::reprocess() handles this.

Bank templates

BankTemplate stores bank-specific layout hints passed to the LLM. Fields:

ColumnTypePurpose
namestringHuman-readable template name
bank_namestringThe bank this template applies to
layout_hintstext / nullFree-text hints passed verbatim to the LLM prompt
is_activebooleanInactive templates are hidden from the upload form

Bank templates are managed directly in the database; there is no UI page for them yet.

Reprocess with hint

Statements in any non-posted status can be reprocessed. The reprocess action opens a modal where the user can enter an optional hint — free text that is appended to the LLM prompt to guide the extraction (e.g. "Debit card fee rows are bank charges, not supplier payments"). The hint is passed through ProcessBankStatementDocument → BankStatementProcessingService::process() → LlmService::extractBankStatement().

Document model fields

Bank statement documents use these Document columns beyond the standard fields:

ColumnNotes
contra_account_idFK to accounts; the GL bank or credit card account (required on upload)
document_numberNullable; extracted from statement or set manually
bank_template_idFK to bank_templates; nullable
llm_confidenceOverall confidence returned by the LLM
metadataJSON: bank_name, account_name, account_number_last4, period_from, period_to, opening_balance, closing_balance, balance_reconciled

Each DocumentLine stores the transaction date, running balance, account reasoning, and invoice match details in its own metadata JSON column.

Permissions

  • documents-view-any / documents-view — to view the list and detail flyout
  • documents-create — to upload statements
  • documents-update — to edit line accounts and reprocess
